name: Graham v. Connor
summary: Excessive force claims analyzed under Fourth Amendment reasonableness standard; objective reasonableness judged from perspective of officer on scene, not with 20/20 hindsight.
holding: All claims that law enforcement used excessive force in course of arrest, investigatory stop, or other seizure analyzed under Fourth Amendment reasonableness standard; assessed from perspective of reasonable officer on scene considering totality of circumstances.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes standard for evaluating use of force; critical for assessing police accountability, excessive force claims, and reasonableness of government enforcement actions.
